payload.title: 42 nd Street Transit Improvements
payload.description: Presentation to Manhattan Community Board 5 Transportation Committee on June 24, 2019 regarding proposed transit improvements on 42nd Street

payload.entity_name: New York City Department of Transportation (NYC DOT)

payload.description: M42 Manhattan Route along 42nd Street
X68 Queens Route using 42nd Street
42nd Street corridor used by M42, X68, and Staten Island express bus routes
Proposed bus lane and curb management improvements on 42nd Street from 8th Avenue to 3rd Avenue, presented to Community Board 5 in June 2019

payload.raw_value_text: Up to 210 buses per hour travel along 42 nd Street

payload.claim_text: On average, M42 buses spend more than 40% of their time stalled in traffic, at red lights, or crawling

warnings: claim.route is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Route ids should link to route records when supported. Suggested relation: route has_claim claim when the route endpoint resolves exactly.

payload.claim_text: Bus running time is unreliable even within the same time period due to varying traffic conditions day to day. Runs can vary by 20-40 minutes, leading to bus bunching, long/unpredictable waits at the bus stop, and crowding on the bus

warnings: claim.route is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Route ids should link to route records when supported. Suggested relation: route has_claim claim when the route endpoint resolves exactly.

payload.claim_text: Bus lanes are underused due to very narrow width (9') and blockages by other vehicles. Quick pick-up/drop-off activity common along corridor. Commercial loading activity is high and durations are short (<1 hr). Turns off 42 St street are difficult due to pedestrian volumes, causing congestion

payload.description: NYC DOT and MTA bus improvement program
Mayor's 2019 State of the City. Improve bus speeds 25% by 2020. Better Buses Action Plan released April 2019. 24 priority projects announced for 2019 to increase bus speeds across all 5 boroughs

Proposed cross section (midblock & intersections with no turns): Curbside bus lane, Thru travel lanes, Offset bus lane, Commercial Metered Parking/Loading. Lane widths: 12' SNB, 11' Travel Lane, 11' Travel Lane, 11' BUS, 9' Parking/Loading. Investigating physical protection in certain segments where feasible

tool_args.label: Proposed bus lane hours and curb regulations

payload.description: Offset bus lanes in effect 24/7. Curbside bus lanes in effect 7 AM - 7 PM all days, except at bus stops or other existing No Standing Anytime. 7 PM - 7 AM hours allocated to metered commercial/truck loading. Other curb space allocated to metered commercial/truck loading, hotel loading zones, authorized parking

Curbside bus lane 7A-7P. 1 hr comm'l metered parking 7P-12A, truck loading 12A-7A. 1 hr comm'l metered parking 7A-12A, truck loading 12A-7A. Bus stop with bus boarder. Curbside bus lane 24/7 (existing No Standing Anytime). Curbside bus lane 7A-7P. No left turns onto 6th Ave 24/7 (existing 7A-7P restriction)

Wide curbside bus lane 7A-7P (allows for passenger pickup/drop-off + buses to pass). 1 hr comm'l metered parking 7P-12A, truck loading 12A-7A. No turns onto 7th Ave. EB left turn split-phase signal (under inv.). Wide curbside bus lane 7A-7P. Move EB bus stop closer to intersection. Painted curb extension

Right turn bay for turns onto 6th Ave. 1 hr comm'l metered parking 7A-12A, truck loading 12A-7A. Bus stop with bus boarder. Curbside bus lane 7A-7P. 1 hr comm'l metered parking 7P-12A, truck loading 12A-7A. Painted curb extension

tool_args.label: Proposed design Madison Ave - Lexington Ave

payload.description: Painted curb extensions. Bus stop with bus boarder. Hotel Loading Zone. Bus signal queue jumps (paired with existing LPI). Curbside bus lane 7A-7P. Curbside bus lane 24/7 (Existing No Standing Anytime). No right turn 7A-7P (existing 7A-10A, 4P-7P) Ex Buses. No left turn 24/7 (existing 7A-7P) Ex Buses
payload.location_text: 42nd Street between Madison Ave and Lexington Ave

payload.description: Bus only left turn lanes. Curbside bus lane No Standing Anytime. Curbside bus lane 7A-7P 1 hr comm'l metered parking 7P-12A, truck loading 12A-7A
payload.location_text: 42nd Street between 5th Ave and Madison Ave

normalized_location.raw_text: 42nd Street between 5th Ave and Madison Ave

payload.description: Turn restriction changes at key intersections: 7th Ave right turn EB to SB - Restricted All Times for All Vehicles (was Permitted All Times). Left turn WB to SB at 7th Ave - Restricted All Times for All Vehicles (was Restricted All Times Except Buses). 6th Ave left turn EB to NB - Restricted All Times for All Vehicles (was No Turns 7am-7pm Except Sunday). Lexington Ave left turn WB to SB - Restricted All Times Except Buses (was No Turns 7am-7pm Except Sunday). Lexington Ave right turn EB to SB - No Turns 7am-7pm Except Buses (was No Turns 7-10am, 4-7pm Mon-Fri Except Buses)

payload.description: Adjustments to plan as needed, street design implementation
Post-implementation data collection & monitoring
Better Buses Action Plan released April 2019. 24 priority projects announced for 2019 to increase bus speeds across all 5 boroughs

payload.claim_text: Current design of 42nd St does not meet the demands of most street users. Street design changes will: Improve bus speeds and reliability; Make commercial metered parking/loading space available throughout the day and overnight to reduce illegal standing and daytime congestion; Improve turns off of 42nd Street at key intersections; Restrict some turns to improve safety and traffic flow

tool_args.label: Bus stop consolidation at Lex/3rd Ave

payload.claim_text: Consolidate Lex Ave and 3rd Ave stops to single stop in each direction to improve stop spacing, more closely matching standard spacing along rest of 42nd
